In reading the FPPC Bulletin for JuIy, 1990, I read on page 21,
paragraph 2, an advice letter to Ralph Grant, your file No. I-90-
144 which said itHusbands and wj-ves are treated as separate persons
with respect to contribution limits. 
However, the Act reguires the
cumulation of contributions where one person directs and controls
the contributions of another person. rl
Although I do not have written advice from FPPC, I called to ask
the guestion of separate contributions from husbands and wives
during the election campaign of L988. At that time I was told
that, 
because of 
Californiars 
community property 
1aws,
contributions from husbands and wives were cumulated. Further,
that having separate bank accounts did not alter that fact and that
the only exception would be an instance where one of the persons
had a totally separate busj-ness in which the spouse had no interest
whatsoever.
If I am interpreting your advice to Ralph Grant correctly, the two
anslrers seem to be in contradiction. 
I'l-1 grant that memory is
imperfect but I believe my memory in this instance is correct
because, ds a woman, I thought it was kind of outrageous!
Will you please set me straight in this matter since I am sure to
be asked during the November campaign and I would like to be
confident that I have the latest, correct answer.
Very truly yours,
A"^fU

sections.question: Will you please set me straight in this matter since I am sure to be asked during the November campaign and I would like to be confident that I have the latest, correct answer.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor seeks clarification on whether contributions from husbands and wives should be treated as separate or cumulated under California political contribution limits, noting an apparent contradiction between prior telephone advice received in 1988 and a published FPPC advice letter (I-90-144) to Ralph Grant.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Incoming request letter with no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The document is formatted as correspondence from the City of Encinitas to FPPC requesting advice. Reference to prior advice letter I-90-144 and telephone advice from 1988 campaign are mentioned. No FPPC response is included in this document.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Will you please set me straight in this matter since I am sure to be asked during the November campaign and I would like to be confident that I have the latest, correct answer.

embedding.summary: City Clerk June Poor requests written clarification from FPPC regarding the treatment of spousal contributions under California contribution limits, citing a contradiction between prior verbal advice (that contributions are cumulated due to community property laws) and a recently published advice letter suggesting husbands and wives are treated as separate persons.
